p1.evidence: When an active embedder is configured, getActiveVectorSourceForQuery selects active_vec.vec_${active.dim} if that table exists (lines 103-110), and attachActiveVectorShard creates that vec_<dim> table on every shard attach (vector-index-store.ts:802-808). Normal index/update paths only write active_vec.vec_memories (vector-index-mutations.ts:325-330 and 599-603), while vector_search joins the selected source table (vector-index-queries.ts:444-449). Startup configures/attaches the active embedder
p1.why_real: In the normal runtime with an active embedder, newly saved successful embeddings are inserted into vec_memories but vector_search reads vec_<dim>, so those records become invisible to vector similarity search until a reindex happens to populate vec_<dim>.
p1.suggested_fix: Either keep active vector reads on active_vec.vec_memories, or make index/update/delete dual-write vec_memories and vec_<dim> atomically. Add an active-embedder regression test where index_memory followed by vector_search returns the inserted row.

p1.title: Integrity health checks can pass while the active vector-search table is empty
p1.evidence: verify_integrity computes missing_vectors and total_vectors only from activeVectorSchema('vec_memories') (lines 1557-1568), but active-embedder vector_search can read active_vec.vec_<dim> instead (lines 103-110 and 444-449). memory_health then reports vecRowsTotal from verifyIntegrity (memory-crud-health.ts:986-990).
p1.why_real: The new degraded-vector health surface can report no missing vectors because vec_memories is populated even when the actual table used by vector_search has no rows, hiding the read-path regression instead of surfacing it.
p1.suggested_fix: Make verify_integrity inspect the same vector source selected for queries, or report and compare both vec_memories and vec_<dim> counts. Add a health test with active embedder state where the two tables diverge.

p1.title: Changelog claims byte-identical ranking parity, but tests prove packed intentionally differs from legacy
p1.evidence: The changelog says packed ranking is `byte-identical to legacy`. The committed test asserts `legacyTop` is not `101`, `packedTop` is `101`, and flat packed weighting is not `101`; the comparison helper only checks packed metrics are at least legacy and `titleSignalRestored`, not byte equality.
p1.why_real: This is a materially false verification claim. Restoring title weighting is expected to change ranking on the oracle fixture.
p1.suggested_fix: Replace the byte-identical claim with the actual gate: packed metrics are at least legacy and restore the title/trigger signal.

p1.title: Implementation-summary verification table still presents the repudiated 111 MB RSS measurement as the P0 budget-gate PASS evidence
p1.evidence: Verification row: 'Current corpus budget fixture | PASS: 10,245 docs, 69.2 MB indexed text, RSS spike 111,017,984 bytes'. The same packet's spec.md §7 (line 139) explicitly repudiates that measurement: 'The original "within budget" evidence (111 MB RSS) was measured against a corpus fixture whose body filler was 100% stop words, so body postings were never indexed' and records the realistic re-validation at 686.8 MB (a breach). The committed fixture (bm25-packed-fixture.ts:19-43) is the post-rep
p1.why_real: This is the canonical PASS evidence for REQ-001, the phase's only P0 requirement, and the phase's own spec declares that evidence invalid. A reader of the implementation summary alone concludes the budget gate passed at 111 MB at this phase, which is false — it breached at this phase and was only br
p1.suggested_fix: Replace the budget-fixture row with the realistic-fixture evidence (686.8 MB breach at phase time) plus a pointer to the phase-017 closure on the original process-RSS metric, and reconcile the continuity block (blockers/recent_action) with spec.md.

p1.title: Implementation summary still claims the RSS gate passed after the phase records it as failed
p1.evidence: implementation-summary.md:52 claims the phase is SHIPPED with budget gates, and line 103 records the current corpus budget as PASS at 111,017,984 bytes. The phase spec contradicts that at spec.md:139, stating realistic re-validation measured a 686.8 MB RSS spike and that the RAM gate test is left failing; the changelog also says Warmup RSS was OVER BUDGET at line 46.
p1.why_real: This materially misstates the shipped phase status and verification evidence. A reader using the implementation summary as the phase record would believe REQ-001 passed when the same phase record says it fired the contingency decision.
p1.suggested_fix: Update implementation-summary.md to match the current phase truth: conditional/shipped-with-open-contingency status, the realistic RSS breach, and the follow-up/phase-017 closure if applicable.

p1.title: Comparison helper ignores the configured NDCG cutoff
p1.evidence: BM25BaselineConfig documents `k` as the top-K cutoff for NDCG and Recall at line 43, and runBM25EngineComparison accepts `config.k` at lines 219-224, but runComparisonMetrics always calls `computeNDCG(evalResults, groundTruth, 10)`.
p1.why_real: Any consumer passing a non-default `k` gets Recall@k but still gets NDCG@10 under the backwards-compatible `ndcg10` field, which can skew regression comparisons and phase evidence.
p1.suggested_fix: Either pass `k` into `computeNDCG` for the comparison helper or split the API so NDCG remains explicitly fixed at 10 while only Recall uses the configurable cutoff.

p1.title: Checkpoint restore skips cache/index invalidation when clearExisting removes data but restores zero rows
p1.evidence: handleCheckpointRestore passes clear_existing into restoreCheckpoint at line 549, but cache clearing, BM25 rebuild, and trigger cache refresh only run when result.restored > 0 or result.workingMemoryRestored > 0 at line 560. The success hints still report 'Previous data was cleared before restore' and 'Search indexes rebuilt' at lines 649-651.
p1.why_real: A clearExisting restore can change the live dataset by deleting existing memory even when the checkpoint contains zero restored rows. In that case stale vector caches, trigger cache, and the existing BM25 singleton can continue serving pre-restore results until another rebuild or restart.
p1.suggested_fix: Run cache invalidation and index reset/rebuild whenever clear_existing is true or any restore count is nonzero. For zero-row restores, clear/reset indexes instead of reporting that they were rebuilt.

p1.title: RAM budget verification is stale and contradicts the current gate
p1.evidence: The summary reports `Current corpus budget fixture | PASS` with RSS spike `111,017,984 bytes`. The same phase spec now says that evidence used stop-word filler and the realistic fixture measured a 686.8 MB warmup RSS spike breach, while the committed test still gates `rssSpike <= RSS_BUDGET_BYTES`.
p1.why_real: REQ-001 is the phase's P0 RAM acceptance gate. Keeping a PASS row in the shipped summary materially misstates release readiness and whether the contingency fired.
p1.suggested_fix: Update the implementation summary to record the realistic-fixture breach/conditional status, or cite the later phase that closed it and remove the stale PASS number.

p1.title: Engine comparison helper can report title weighting restored when packed ranking is identical to legacy
p1.evidence: `titleSignalRestored: packed.hitRate1 >= legacy.hitRate1` treats equality as restored. The fixture includes a deliberate body-noise competitor in bm25-packed-fixture.ts:106-109, so a regression that flattens packed scoring like legacy can still satisfy this flag when both engines miss or both tie on hitRate1.
p1.why_real: The exported comparison result is a pass/fail signal for the phase's BM25F claim. As written, it can hide the exact regression it is supposed to catch: packed field weights not affecting ranking.
p1.suggested_fix: Make `titleSignalRestored` a targeted per-query assertion, for example require the weighted packed search for `auth guard` to rank memory 101 first and the flat/legacy path not to, or return per-query title-signal diagnostics instead of aggregate hitRate equality.

p1.title: FakeVectorStore accepts vector searches the real port rejects or filters differently
p1.evidence: FakeVectorStore.search defaults the filter to `options.minScore ?? Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY` and `cosineSimilarity` only compares `Math.min(left.length, right.length)`, while BetterSqliteVectorStore validates exact embedding dimension and defaults the port overload's missing `minScore` to 0. The contract suite always passes `minScore: 0`, so it never exercises the default or a dimension mismatch.
p1.why_real: Tests using the fake can pass with wrong-dimensional embeddings or negative-score matches that the real adapter would reject or suppress, undermining the phase's fake-substitution and contract-test guarantee.
p1.suggested_fix: Make FakeVectorStore enforce the same embedding-dimension rule and default `minScore` behavior as the better-sqlite port, then add contract cases for omitted `minScore` and mismatched embedding dimensions.

p1.title: FakeContentionPolicy skips configured backoff hooks
p1.evidence: FakeContentionPolicy computes `delayMs` and calls `onRetry`, then immediately checks `shouldAbort` without invoking `options.sleep` or `options.sleepSync`. The real BetterSqliteContentionPolicy awaits/synchronously runs the configured sleep hooks, and file-watcher retry consumers pass a custom abortable sleep.
p1.why_real: A fake-based integration test cannot validate abortable retry sleeps or sync backoff behavior, so regressions in retry shutdown/backoff semantics can be hidden while the port contract still passes.
p1.suggested_fix: Honor `sleep`/`sleepSync` in FakeContentionPolicy, including the `sync` path, and add a contract test that proves the sleep hook runs before the next attempt and before the post-delay abort check.

p1.title: code-index and skill-advisor stale guards are not initialized by their documented builds
p1.evidence: code-index computes a source hash, accepts only a stored hash match, then falls back to comparing watched source mtimes to the single entrypoint dist mtime at lines 94-103; its build is only `tsc --build tsconfig.json` (`.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/package.json:7`) and never writes `.code-index-cli-source-hash.json`. skill-advisor has the same runtime-only hash/write pattern at `.opencode/bin/skill-advisor.cjs:86-95`, while its build/postbuild only compiles/copies data (`.opencode/skills/
p1.why_real: A schema/manifest/tool-descriptor change can be correctly rebuilt by TypeScript without rewriting the CLI entrypoint JS, leaving the source newer than `dist/*-cli.js` and no build-written hash to prove freshness. The shims can then keep exiting 69 after the documented rebuild command, breaking the C
p1.suggested_fix: Add code-index and skill-advisor build finalizers that write the same source-hash state files after successful builds, or compare each watched source to its corresponding dist output instead of only the entrypoint. Add a regression test that changes/touches a watched schema or manifest, runs the doc

p1.title: Byte-identical ranking claim is not backed by the shipped parity tests
p1.evidence: The summary claims ranking output is byte-identical to before. The shipped test only compares warmed vs direct results from the same current packed implementation at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:190-197, and the legacy comparison only asserts packed MRR is >= legacy at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:283-285. There is no frozen pre-change golden ordering/score oracle in the reviewed test file.
p1.why_real: This is a materially stronger shipped verification claim than the tests support. The current tests prove internal current-engine consistency and a baseline MRR condition, not byte-identical scores/order versus the pre-change implementation.
p1.suggested_fix: Either add a frozen golden parity test for representative pre-change packed outputs, or soften the docs to state that warmed and direct current packed paths match and the packed-vs-legacy MRR baseline still passes.

p1.title: Complete spec still says packed-postings layout was frozen/out of scope after the implementation changed it
p1.evidence: The complete spec says ranking/scoring semantics and the packed-postings layout are frozen, and line 107 repeats that the packed-postings layout is out of scope. The implementation summary says the phase replaced mutable-to-six-array finalization with no-copy chunked packed postings and typed width promotion, and bm25-index.ts:121-128 defines the new chunked typed posting store.
p1.why_real: The shipped spec contradicts the delivered code and the phase summary. Readers relying on the completed spec would believe the layout was not changed, which is materially false for this phase's actual implementation.
p1.suggested_fix: Update the completed spec scope/out-of-scope text to record the operator-approved packed-layout change and its parity gate, rather than leaving the original frozen-layout constraint in place.

p1.title: Packed incremental indexing collapses production save/update rows to body-only text, dropping title and trigger field boosts
p1.evidence: BM25Index.addDocument() routes packed-inmemory calls to addDocumentFields(id, { body: text }) at lines 508-511. Production consumers still call addDocument(String(id), buildBm25DocumentText(...)), e.g. handlers/save/create-record.ts lines 417-425 and handlers/memory-crud-update.ts lines 385-395, while the field-weighting test uses addDocumentFields() directly via addFixtureDocuments() at tests/bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts lines 39-43 and 212-233.
p1.why_real: Rows indexed by startup rebuild/syncChangedRows preserve title/trigger/file/body fields, but rows saved or updated after warmup are indexed as a single body field. Those documents lose the intended 10x title and 5x trigger boosts until a full rebuild, so ranking behavior differs by ingestion path an
p1.suggested_fix: Update production mutation consumers to call addDocumentFields(String(id), buildBm25DocumentFields(row)) or add a field-aware public helper, then add an integration test that a saved/updated title hit ranks the same as the same row indexed through rebuildFromDatabase/syncChangedRows.

p1.title: RSS budget gate tests sync warmup path, not production async batched path
p1.evidence: Test iterates `iterateCorpusSizedDocuments()` synchronously calling `addDocumentFields` in a tight loop (line 122-128). Production warmup uses `rebuildFromDatabase` (bm25-index.ts:760) which batches via `syncChangedRows` with async `setTimeout(..., 0)` between batches. The implementation-summary.md claims 'peak-sampled RSS spike 136.5 MB ≤ 150 MB' (line 91) and changelog says 'always-on gate' (line 35), but the gate only asserts the sync path.
p1.why_real: The async batched path yields to the event loop between batches, giving V8 opportunities to run GC and decommit transient pages. The sync path holds all transient allocations without yielding. The 136.5 MB measurement may understate or overstate the production spike; the hard gate cannot catch a reg
p1.suggested_fix: Add a test that uses `rebuildFromDatabase` (with `vi.useFakeTimers` + `vi.runAllTimersAsync`) and measures peak RSS across batch boundaries, or document that the sync-path RSS is a conservative upper bound with evidence.

p1.title: Read-only embedding load path still runs schema ensure logic
p1.evidence: getDbReadOnly promises recommend-path access never creates the database or runs schema migrations (lines 404-411) and opens the handle readonly (line 423), but the active-embedder branch calls ensureVecTableForDim(database, active.dim) before reading vectors (line 1245). The same helper is used from ensureSchemaMigrations (lines 303-306).
p1.why_real: A secondary/bridge recommend process with an active embedder pointer can attempt DDL through a readonly handle or throw instead of degrading gracefully, breaking semantic shadow scoring on the read path.
p1.suggested_fix: Remove schema-ensure calls from loadSkillEmbeddings; check for the expected vec table read-only and return an empty embedding set with a warning if it is absent.

p1.title: Dead-socket respawn can signal a reused, unrelated PID
p1.evidence: resolveRespawnTargetPid trusts persisted childPid/ownerPid from the lease file (lines 584-587), then reapOwnerBeforeRespawn sends SIGTERM/SIGKILL to that PID (lines 404-418) with no executable, start-time, or socket-owner validation.
p1.why_real: If a stale lease remains after the advisor process exits and the OS reuses that PID, the respawn path can terminate an unrelated same-user process. This is a real concurrent stale-state hazard and can cause data loss outside the advisor daemon.
p1.suggested_fix: Persist child process identity evidence in the lease, such as executable path plus start time/ctime where available, and verify it before sending signals; otherwise refuse respawn and report the stale lease.

p1.title: Absolute advisor DB override is joined under workspaceRoot and ignored
p1.evidence: SKILL_GRAPH_DB becomes join(advisorDbDirOverride, 'skill-graph.sqlite') when MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR or SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR is set (lines 41-44), but loadSqliteProjection then computes join(workspaceRoot, SKILL_GRAPH_DB) (line 272). In Node, path.join('/repo', '/tmp/db/skill-graph.sqlite') normalizes to '/repo/tmp/db/skill-graph.sqlite', not the override path.
p1.why_real: Any absolute sandbox or operator DB override makes the recommend path look in the wrong location, fall back to filesystem projection, and silently drop SQLite-only data such as indexed doc triggers and graph state.
p1.suggested_fix: Use the override path as-is when SKILL_GRAPH_DB is absolute, for example path.isAbsolute(SKILL_GRAPH_DB) ? SKILL_GRAPH_DB : join(workspaceRoot, SKILL_GRAPH_DB).

p1.title: Launcher strips two shipped advisor feature flags, so operator-gated features cannot be enabled through the normal MCP path
p1.evidence: `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` controls the child process environment and includes `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS` but omits `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_BM25_LEXICAL_SHADOW` and `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_FEEDBACK_CALIBRATION_SHADOW`; `createChildEnv` later filters child env strictly through that allowlist. The phase docs claim the BM25 lexical shadow helper and feedback calibration ship default-off/operator-gated via those env flags.
p1.why_real: The features can pass direct Vitest/env tests but are not actually enableable in the launched advisor daemon because the launcher drops their flags before spawning the MCP server. That makes the shipped default-off rollout claims materially false for the normal runtime path.
p1.suggested_fix: Add the two advisor feature flags to `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` and add/extend a launcher env-pass-through test so default-off feature flags remain operator-gated in the daemon path.

p1.title: advisor_recommend persists raw user prompts in the default shadow log
p1.evidence: Lines 378-387 call recordShadowDelta with `prompt: input.prompt` for each shadow recommendation. The sink appends the whole record as JSONL to the default `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/data/shadow-deltas.jsonl`, so prompt text containing secrets is stored persistently even though response attribution is sanitized.
p1.why_real: This is a recommend-path side effect and a privacy/security regression: users commonly paste credentials, tokens, private paths, or proprietary text into prompts, and the shipped prompt-safe response contract does not prevent those raw prompts from being written to disk.
p1.suggested_fix: Do not store raw prompts by default. Store a keyed hash or redacted/canonical feature summary instead, and gate raw prompt logging behind an explicit debug-only opt-in with clear retention handling.

p1.title: Absolute skill graph DB overrides are resolved under the workspace instead of used directly
p1.evidence: Lines 41-44 allow `SKILL_GRAPH_DB` to be built from `MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR` / `SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR`, but line 272 always does `join(workspaceRoot, SKILL_GRAPH_DB)`. With an absolute override such as `/tmp/db`, Node's `path.join('/repo', '/tmp/db/skill-graph.sqlite')` resolves to `/repo/tmp/db/skill-graph.sqlite`, not `/tmp/db/skill-graph.sqlite`.
p1.why_real: The launcher and DB layer both advertise/support DB directory overrides, and temp-fixture/sandboxed DBs are a repo convention. Under an absolute override, the projection silently misses the live SQLite graph and falls back to filesystem projection, dropping graph edges and doc-trigger rows from reco
p1.suggested_fix: Resolve the DB path with an absolute-path guard, for example `isAbsolute(SKILL_GRAPH_DB) ? SKILL_GRAPH_DB : join(workspaceRoot, SKILL_GRAPH_DB)`, or share the existing DB resolver used by the skill-graph DB layer.

p1.title: Adopting a responsive stale child leaves the secondary proxy holding the owner lease
p1.evidence: checkStrictSingleWriter acquires the owner lease and starts the heartbeat before stale launcher-lease handling at lines 702-709. In the stale live child branch, a successful bridge returns true at lines 733-735 without clearing that owner lease; only the non-bridge/non-respawn branch clears it at lines 737-740.
p1.why_real: During a long bridged session, the owner lease points at the secondary proxy process rather than the actual advisor daemon. Later launchers take the owner-lease path and leaseResultForOwnerLease can synthesize a no-socket lease for that proxy, so additional sessions can be blocked from bridging to t
p1.suggested_fix: When the stale child is adopted via bridge, release the temporary owner lease and heartbeat before returning, or transfer ownership to a lease record that accurately points at a bridgeable daemon/socket. Keep the current owner lease only for the respawn path that actually launches the replacement ch

p1.title: Reap helper can allow replacement spawn without confirming the old child exited
p1.evidence: `reapOwnerBeforeRespawn` sends SIGKILL and awaits `waitForPidExit(ownerPid, 1000)`, but ignores the boolean result and returns `{ allowed: true }` at line 420. Both respawn paths rely on that allowed result before replacement launch (`respawnAfterDeadSocket` around line 629 and stale bootstrap cleanup around line 1202), while the spec/summary claim the old child is reaped before replacement spawn.
p1.why_real: If a stale or wedged child remains live past the grace window, the launcher can proceed to spawn a replacement while the old process is still alive, weakening the single-writer/resource-safety guarantee this phase claims to close.
p1.suggested_fix: After the SIGKILL wait, recheck liveness and return `allowed:false` or report without spawning when the old pid is still alive; add a test that exercises the non-exiting reap path.

p1.title: Launcher drops daemon trust-default env, breaking native trusted mutations
p1.evidence: CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST lines 85-119 omits MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT, and createChildEnv only forwards allowlisted variables at lines 228-231. The committed MCP config sets MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT=trusted, while advisor-server.ts resolves native trust from process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT.
p1.why_real: The advisor child is spawned with env: createChildEnv(), so the daemon never receives the committed trust grant. Native MCP callers without _meta are then fail-closed as untrusted, causing advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan, and apply-mode skill_graph_propagate_enhances to be rejected despite the shi
p1.suggested_fix: Add MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_TRUST_DEFAULT to the launcher child env allowlist and add a launcher/env regression test that asserts committed MCP env keys required by advisor-server.ts reach the spawned daemon.

p1.title: Dead-socket respawn can abort after killing the old daemon when model-server cleanup is slow
p1.evidence: respawnAfterDeadSocket waits only 1000ms for previousOwner.ownerPid before trying writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive at lines 636-653. The old launcher's child-exit path awaits shutdownModelServerForLauncherExit before clearing leases at lines 1114-1124, and shutdownModelServerRoot can wait the 7000ms grace path at lines 921-945.
p1.why_real: With SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_MODEL_SERVER_ENABLED=1 and a slow model-server root shutdown, the respawner can SIGTERM the old advisor child, time out after 1s while the old launcher still owns the lease, fail to claim the owner lease, and return LEASE_HELD instead of serving the requesting client's ini
p1.suggested_fix: Wait for the previous owner lease to clear for the same shutdown grace budget used by model-server cleanup, or explicitly coordinate owner-lease transfer under the bootstrap lock; add a sandbox test with delayed model-server shutdown during dead-socket respawn.

p1.title: Interrupted reindex can publish a shard containing only the tail rows
p1.evidence: runJob resumes from the persisted processed count (line 535), deletes any staging shard at start (line 567), selects the next batch with OFFSET processed (line 579), writes only those remaining rows to the staging shard (line 591), then renames that staging file over the active shard (line 623).
p1.why_real: After a crash or restore pause with processed > 0, the resumed job starts from an empty staging file but skips the already-processed prefix. Completion then publishes a shard missing those vectors, while the job can still be marked completed and repair sentinels cleared, causing durable recall loss.
p1.suggested_fix: When staging is recreated, rebuild from offset 0 and reset progress, or preserve and validate the existing staging shard before resuming from processed. Do not swap a staging shard unless its rowcount covers the intended completed dataset.

p1.title: Repair de-duplication does not survive process restart
p1.evidence: repair context is stored only in the in-memory vectorShardRepairJobs map (lines 123 and 742-744). resumeReindexJobs reloads queued/running jobs from embedder_jobs but does not rebuild that map (lines 777-792), and startVectorShardRepairReindex de-dupes only by scanning the map (lines 809-821).
p1.why_real: After a crash with a queued/running repair job persisted, a restart can resume the old job and also schedule a new repair from the sentinel because the same-shard guard has forgotten the old repair context. That violates the claimed one same-shard in-flight repair invariant and can race duplicate sh
p1.suggested_fix: Persist repair metadata with the job row or a side table, reload it during resume, and de-dupe against persisted queued/running repair jobs before creating a new repair job.

p1.title: Marker-less incomplete shards still attach as healthy
p1.evidence: assess_vector_shard_repair_state computes shardComplete from success-count versus vector rows (lines 635-642), but handle_vector_shard_repair_assessment only acts when a sentinel exists or orphan quarantine artifacts exist (lines 677-691). attachActiveVectorShard then attaches/creates the shard and ensures schema anyway (lines 1562-1585).
p1.why_real: If the active shard is missing or incomplete without a sentinel/quarantine artifact, including after the interrupted-reindex tail-shard case, the system serves an incomplete active vector shard instead of scheduling repair. That contradicts the completeness-oracle invariant and silently degrades rec
p1.suggested_fix: Treat a non-complete shard with expected successful rows as degraded even without a sentinel, and schedule a repair with a distinct reason such as incomplete shard without sentinel. Keep expected == 0 as the safe empty-database case.

p1.title: Completeness check can clear repair intent when vector IDs are incomplete
p1.evidence: assess_vector_shard_repair_state treats a shard as complete when probe.ok and vecRows >= successful memory rows. It never verifies that every successful memory_index id exists in the vector table; adjacent mutation paths update active_vec.vec_memories only, not vec_<dim>, while active non-default vector queries join memory_index to vec_<dim> by id.
p1.why_real: A shard with equal counts but the wrong IDs, for example one stale extra vector row and one missing successful memory id, passes this oracle. The stale sentinel is then cleared at line 679 and no repair runs, leaving the missing successful memory unreturned by vector retrieval despite the repair-int
p1.suggested_fix: Replace the rowcount-only oracle with an ID-coverage check against the selected vector table and require zero successful memory rows missing a vector row. Add a regression fixture with equal counts but one missing successful id plus one stale extra vector id.

p1.title: Strict-validation PASS claim relies on untracked metadata files
p1.evidence: The summary claims strict spec validation passed, but `git cat-file -e HEAD:<phase>/description.json` and `git cat-file -e HEAD:<phase>/graph-metadata.json` both report the paths exist on disk but not in HEAD. The committed phase tree contains spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md, implementation-summary.md, and review artifacts, but not those metadata files.
p1.why_real: The user asked for the current committed state. The validation claim is a shipped truth claim, but the committed phase is missing required metadata files; a clean checkout would not match the working-tree validation state.
p1.suggested_fix: Commit the generated `description.json` and `graph-metadata.json` for this phase, or revise the completion/validation claims until committed-state validation is actually green.

p1.title: filePath fallback heuristic classifies human saves as 'import', stripping manual-overwrite protection from rows in index/scan-named spec paths
p1.evidence: deriveSourceKindFromContext falls back to `/\b(import|ingest|scan|index)\b/i.test(filePath)` -> 'import' (write-provenance.ts:89-93). memory-save.ts:2271-2276 always includes `filePath: routedFilePath` in writeProvenance, and pe-gating.ts:228/344 merge `filePath: parsed.filePath`; for a plain human memory_save (no provenanceSource/actor, tool='memory_save' matches no label, empty scope) the path heuristic is the deciding rule. Verified against real repo paths: `.../003-memory-index-causal-lifecy
p1.why_real: The genuine import paths never need this heuristic: scan sets provenanceSource 'memory_index_scan' explicitly (memory-save.ts:2857-2864) and async ingest sets defaults at its persist site, so the only writes the filePath branch actually decides are untagged (human-default) saves whose document path
p1.suggested_fix: Remove the filePath branch from deriveSourceKindFromContext (or restrict it to call sites whose origin is genuinely scan/ingest, where explicit provenanceSource already exists), so an untagged write truly defaults to 'human' as the changelog claims; add a regression test that an empty/path-only cont

p1.title: Phase marked Complete while its own verification table and checklist record 4 of 5 handoff gates as never run
p1.evidence: implementation-summary.md metadata says Status: Complete / Completed 2026-06-11 (line 46), yet its Verification table (lines 117-121) lists tsc, the provenance/guard vitest suite, validate.sh --strict, and comment-hygiene as 'Pending final run', and checklist.md:77/81 still says 'To be filled after final run'. spec.md:46 declares Status Complete with continuity completion_pct 100, while spec.md:52 Handoff Criteria require exactly those checks to pass. The changelog meanwhile asserts 'Provenance
p1.why_real: Three shipped docs make irreconcilable claims about the same completion state (summary: pending; spec: complete; changelog: passed), which directly violates the phase's own handoff criteria and the repo's completion-metadata reconciliation rule. This is a truthfulness defect in shipped docs even tho
p1.suggested_fix: Fill the implementation-summary verification table and checklist evidence rows with the actual command results (tsc exit 0, vitest pass counts, validate.sh output, hygiene check), or downgrade Status from Complete until they are recorded.

p1.title: Changelog materially overstates guard enforcement
p1.evidence: The changelog says provenance injection means the write-ingress guard enforces the constitutional rule that automated writers never overwrite manual or constitutional memory, while the phase spec and implementation summary explicitly document same-path retire and auto-promotion guard gaps as follow-ons.
p1.why_real: This is a shipped-truthfulness issue: readers can conclude guard enforcement is complete when the phase itself says two P0-class guard-coverage gaps remain open.
p1.suggested_fix: Narrow the changelog claim to provenance tagging and covered guard paths, and explicitly state that same-path retire and auto-promotion enforcement remain follow-on work.

p1.title: Caller-supplied provenance labels are persisted without secret scrubbing
p1.evidence: WriteProvenanceContext accepts provenanceSource/provenanceActor as unknown caller input, then buildPostInsertProvenanceFields normalizes those strings and returns them verbatim for persistence; persistProvenanceMetadata writes those fields into memory_index.
p1.why_real: A caller can accidentally or maliciously place credentials in provenanceSource/provenanceActor, and the committed helper stores them directly in durable metadata columns. That is a real security/data-retention bug in the provenance write path.
p1.suggested_fix: Scrub normalized provenance_source and provenance_actor before returning them from buildPostInsertProvenanceFields, and add a regression test that secret-like provenance labels are redacted before persistence.

p1.title: Completion is claimed while required final verification is still pending
p1.evidence: The phase summary marks Status as Complete, but the same file still says final TypeScript, provenance/guard suite, strict validation, and comment-hygiene checks are Pending final run at lines 118-121; line 16 also says the next safe action is to record those final results after they run.
p1.why_real: The shipped docs present completion/release-readiness while their own verification table says required handoff checks have not run. That is a materially false shipped claim, independent of code behavior.
p1.suggested_fix: Either run and record the final verification evidence, or downgrade the phase status/completion metadata until those checks are actually complete.

p1.title: PE reinforce can strip manual-row protection by overwriting source_kind
p1.evidence: reinforceExistingMemory applies caller provenance directly to the existing memoryId after updating the row. The new test seeds the row as human and asserts the reinforced row becomes source_kind: 'system' at tests/pe-gating-provenance.vitest.ts:188-205.
p1.why_real: source_kind is also the protection signal: memory-crud-update treats human/null rows as protected and deliberately preserves source_kind='human' for automated updates while only recording provenance metadata. Flipping a human row to agent/system during a harmless PE reinforce makes later automated p
p1.suggested_fix: Do not overwrite source_kind for protected existing rows during reinforce; preserve human/null/constitutional protection while persisting provenance_source/provenance_actor, or add a separate last-writer provenance field. Add a regression that a human row reinforced by an automated save remains prot

p1.title: implementation-summary.md documents the pre-remediation state, not what shipped
p1.evidence: Verification table claims 'Flag-on idempotency/near-duplicate suite after fixes | PASS ... 10 tests passed', but the shipped test file contains 12 tests (memory-idempotency-and-near-duplicate.vitest.ts has 12 it() blocks, including 'signals won/lost on store so a concurrent loser replays the winner by key' at line 122). The What Was Built section (lines 53-72) lists only three fixes and never mentions the shipped concurrent-loser replay path (memory-save.ts:3607-3618), the new lookupIdempotencyR
p1.why_real: The implementation-summary is the canonical completion record (the resume ladder reads it first), its continuity block says 'Phase complete; preserve final verification evidence', yet its verification evidence describes an earlier code state. The two shipped records conflict on what shipped and what
p1.suggested_fix: Regenerate/patch implementation-summary.md to reflect the post-remediation state: 12/12 test result, the lookupIdempotencyReceiptByKey addition, the boolean store contract, and the concurrent-loser replay behavior in memory-save.ts.

p1.title: Lost receipt-store conflicts fall through to a successful divergent response
p1.evidence: `lookupIdempotencyReceiptByKey` can return `conflict` when the stored payload hash differs (`idempotency-receipts.ts` lines 136-138), but the lost-store path only handles `winner.status === 'replay'` at lines 3615-3618 and otherwise falls through to `return response` at line 3625.
p1.why_real: Concurrent same-key requests with a genuine payload difference can both miss before storage; the loser can mutate, lose receipt insertion, detect a conflict by key, and still return its own success response instead of the documented idempotency conflict.
p1.suggested_fix: Handle the post-store `conflict` branch explicitly by returning the same `idempotency_key_conflict` error as the pre-save lookup path, and pair it with pre-mutation reservation or rollback so the conflicting mutation is not committed.

p1.title: Idempotency receipt is created only after the mutating save has already run
p1.evidence: The handler performs the receipt lookup before the save at lines 3445-3468, then runs `indexMemoryFile` at lines 3521-3528, and only stores the receipt after building the response at lines 3607-3610.
p1.why_real: Two concurrent same-key requests can both observe a miss before either receipt exists, then both proceed into the mutating save path. The later `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` only protects the receipt table and can at most change the loser's returned response; it does not prevent or roll back the loser's
p1.suggested_fix: Reserve the idempotency key before the mutating save, or move the miss/check/store decision under the same lock/transaction so only one request can execute the mutation and losers wait/replay before writing.

p1.title: Flag-off suite verification is overstated because the test file forces the flag on
p1.evidence: The summary claims a flag-off idempotency/near-duplicate suite passed, and spec.md requires running the test file with the flag enabled and unset. But the test file's beforeEach unconditionally sets process.env.SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY = 'true' at memory-idempotency-and-near-duplicate.vitest.ts:66-68, so an externally unset flag run would still execute most tests as flag-on.
p1.why_real: The shipped verification evidence implies full default-off regression coverage, but code reality only has targeted assertions for isMemoryIdempotencyEnabled default behavior and one near-duplicate flag-off case. That materially overstates the completed handoff criteria.
p1.suggested_fix: Either add a real flag-off describe/parameterized run that does not force the env var on and covers the save path, or narrow the implementation summary and checklist language to the specific default-helper and near-duplicate assertions that actually exist.

p1.title: Concurrent same-key memory_save losers can still return non-idempotent duplicate/unchanged responses
p1.evidence: The handler does the receipt lookup before indexing at lines 3445-3468, but after indexing it only enters the lost-store replay path when shouldStoreMemorySaveReceipt(result, response) is true at line 3608. That guard only allows indexed/updated/deferred results in idempotency-receipts.ts:104-113, so a concurrent identical request that missed the pre-lookup but then hits the normal duplicate/unchanged dedup path never re-checks the winner receipt and returns the duplicate/unchanged response inst
p1.why_real: This violates the phase claim that a concurrent first-write loser replays the winner and breaks response-stable idempotency when SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY is enabled. The added test exercises storeIdempotencyReceipt directly, but not the actual handleMemorySave path where duplicate/unchanged losers
p1.suggested_fix: Keep the idempotency lookup, mutation, and receipt store/replay decision in one critical section or add a post-save receipt re-check for same-key duplicate/unchanged losers. Also handle a post-store conflict result explicitly instead of falling through to the loser response.
